title: Customer address invalid
steps:
Customer address invalid.
Team chat says postcode missing.
Fix:
Add postcode manually,
evalidate address,
Save and continue booking.
author: RPA Bot

title: Error Code AUTH_401
summary: User not authorized to access module.
steps:
Error Code: AUTH_401
User not authorized to access module.
Resolution:
- Check AD group access
- User must be in OPS_FWD_ROLE
- Logout and login again
author: RPA Bot

title: Label not printing properly
steps:
Label not printing properly.
Usually printer cache issue.
Restart printer service,
Clear queue,
Reprint label.
If still blur, change toner.
author: RPA Bot

title: New staff setup steps
summary: New staff setup steps and common delay reasons.
steps:
﻿New staff setup steps:
✔ Create AD account
✔ Give email access
✔ SAP + CW1 role
✔ Share SOP folder
Delay usually because SAP request not approved
author: RPA Bot

title: POD upload failed again
summary: POD upload failed due to excessive file size. Compress and refresh to fix it.
steps:
POD upload failed again 😓
Error msg: “File size too large”
Just compress the image before uploading.
JPEG works better than PNG.
After upload, refresh screen twice.
cxxx
author: RPA Bot

title: POD upload failed again
summary: This procedure outlines how to resolve a "file size too large" error when uploading Proof of Delivery (POD) documents to the system.
steps:
1. Compress the image file to reduce its size.
2. Convert the image to JPEG format instead of PNG for better compatibility.
3. Upload the compressed JPEG file.
4. Refresh the screen twice after the upload is complete.
5. xxx
author: john

title: Problem with PC
summary: This procedure provides a troubleshooting guide to resolve hardware or software conflicts that prevent a computer from completing its shutdown sequence.
steps:
1. Close all open applications and save any unsaved work.
2. Open Task Manager (Ctrl+Shift+Esc) to identify and "End Task" on any programs that are not responding.
3. Check for any active Windows Updates that may be processing in the background.
4. Perform a forced shutdown by pressing and holding the physical power button for 10 seconds.
5. Disconnect any external USB peripherals or docking stations and attempt a normal restart.
6. Disable "Fast Startup" in the Windows Power Options settings to prevent kernel hibernation issues.
7. Contact the DHL IT Service Desk if the issue persists to check for BIOS updates or hardware failure.
author: john

title: POD upload failed again
summary: This procedure outlines the troubleshooting steps for resolving "File size too large" errors when uploading Proof of Delivery (POD) images.
steps:
1. Compress the image file to reduce its size.
2. Convert the image to JPEG format for optimal compatibility.
3. Upload the compressed JPEG file.
4. Refresh the screen twice to verify the upload success.
author: john
